entries.notes: Very high Wikipedia pageviews reflect Bacopa monnieri's dual use as a medicinal/nootropic herb (Ayurvedic use) beyond the aquarium hobby. Aquarium relevance is solid. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.

Primarily a terrestrial/bog plant (Lysimachia nummularia 'Aurea') that can grow submerged. High Wikipedia pageviews reflect its widespread use as a garden groundcover. Aquarium use is valid but it is not exclusively aquatic.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Wikipedia pageviews include garden/hummingbird interest beyond aquarium use.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Wikipedia pageviews are from genus-level article. Multiple varieties carried by Buce Plant; representative species is Anubias barteri. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Also known as Ambulia. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on AquariumPlants.com. Tropica carries V. americana under 'Natans' and 'Gigantea' varieties.

Buce Plant product is listed as Ceratopteris cornuta (a related species/synonym). Tropica carries C. thalictroides. AquariumPlants.com sells as 'Water Sprite Laceleaf'. Wikipedia article is for C. thalictroides.

Genus-level entry representing 60+ named Bucephalandra cultivars sold by Buce Plant. Tropica carries Kedagang, pygmaea, sp. Red, and Needle Leaf varieties. AquariumPlants.com has 15+ Buce varieties. Wikipedia pageviews from genus article.
Listed as Echinodorus bleheri on Buce Plant and Tropica; taxonomically reclassified as E. grisebachii 'Bleherae'. Confirmed on AquariumPlants.com as 'Amazon Sword'.
Confirmed on Tropica (as Salvinia minima, close relative) and AquariumPlants.com.
Formerly known as Vesicularia dubyana. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica. AquariumPlants.com sells as 'Hydrocotyle Japan'.
Confirmed on Tropica (article) and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Multiple color varieties (red, green, blood red, orange juice) sold by Buce Plant.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Primarily a terrestrial/terrarium plant. Used in paludariums and very high humidity setups, not truly submerged. AquariumPlants.com does not list it.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Known as HC Cuba. One of the smallest aquarium plants. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Vesicularia ferriei; Wikipedia article linked to genus-level Vesicularia dubyana article. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Multiple varieties (broad leaf, coffeefolia, striped, etc.).
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Multiple color forms (brown, green, tropica) available.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Multiple cultivars: mini, rosanervig, rosaefolia, bronze.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Madagascar Lace Plant. Confirmed on Tropica.
Carnivorous bladderwort used as a carpeting plant. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Wikipedia pageviews from genus-level Schismatoglottis article. Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Also known as Congo Fern.
Also sold as Hemianthus micranthemoides. Confirmed on Tropica.
Also sold as Elodea densa (older trade name). Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com as Anacharis Densa.
Confirmed on Tropica. AquariumPlants.com carries Cabomba caroliniana (related species).
Also known as Eusteralis stellata. Confirmed on Tropica. AquariumPlants.com carries it.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica. Often confused with Rotala rotundifolia in trade.
Floating plant. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.

Mermaid Weed. Confirmed on Tropica.

Phoenix Moss. Confirmed on Tropica.
Includes var. verticillata 'Pantanal' and 'Cuba' variants. Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Tiger variety. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Small Borneo aroid genus closely related to Bucephalandra. Multiple varieties: Narrow, Caulecens Sabertooth, White Fang, Nakamotoi. Confirmed via Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica (article) and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Also known as American Yellow-cress or Lakecress. Confirmed on Tropica.

Borneo aroid, Wikipedia article at genus level. Confirmed on Tropica.
Listed on Buce Plant as Anubias glabra; Tropica catalogs it as Anubias barteri var. glabra (synonym). Confirmed on Tropica.

Stargrass. Confirmed on Tropica.
Water Wisteria. No English Wikipedia article. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.

Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Widely used carpeting plant.
No English Wikipedia article. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. One of the most popular carpeting plants.
No standalone English Wikipedia article (Christmas moss article is a stub redirect). Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Multiple Homalomena species (Sp. Sekadau South, Broad Sekadau South, Narrow Sekadau South, Humilis, Insignis, Silver). Wikipedia pageviews from genus article. Confirmed on Tropica.
Confirmed on Tropica.
Parrot's Feather. Confirmed on Tropica.
Sold as Nymphaea tiger lotus or N. zenkeri in the hobby; taxonomically related to N. lotus. Wikipedia article used is for N. lotus. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Multiple forms: super red, red, sphaerocarpa.
Buce Plant sells compact form as 'Dwarf Nomaphila Siamensis'. Confirmed on Tropica.

Water Poppy. Confirmed on Tropica.
Formerly Echinodorus tenellus. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.
No English Wikipedia article. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com. Multiple variants: green, mini red, mini pink, yellow, bangladesh.
Confirmed on AquariumPlants.com.
Sold as C. balansae in trade; scientifically classified as C. crispatula var. balansae. Confirmed on Tropica and AquariumPlants.com.

A named cultivar/hybrid. Confirmed on AquariumPlants.com.
Also known as Ludwigia Guinea. Confirmed on Tropica.

Multiple Eriocaulon species on Buce Plant: Feather Duster, sp Vietnam, sp Quinquangulare Red, Cinereum. No Wikipedia article for aquarium species. Confirmed on Tropica.

Water pepper; a marginal/emergent plant that can be grown partially submerged. Primarily used in paludarium settings. Confirmed on Tropica. Wikipedia article focused on terrestrial use.
